thoughts.label: TWO POSITIONS IN STOP ALERT ZONE
thoughts.text: Pre-market tech risk-off pushed both NVDA (-3.69% from entry) and MRVL (-4.44%) past their -3% alert thresholds. Neither has hit the -5% exit yet — NVDA sits 1.4% above its $190.04 stop, MRVL just 0.6% above its $265.08 stop. Fractional sells cannot execute in pre-market (regular-hours only); the between-session monitor and the 9 AM session carry the watch.

thoughts.label: SIGNAL ENGINE DID NOT RUN
thoughts.text: signal_matrix.json still reads AWAITING_FIRST_RUN — the 5:50 AM GitHub Action did not populate. No RSI/MACD/Z-score/composite data this session. Compound Entry Gate cannot be computed; all entry evaluations fall back to standard analysis and are deferred. The signal_matrix card is intentionally empty until the engine's first run.

thoughts.label: ZERO BUYING POWER — NO TRADES
thoughts.text: Buying power is $0.00, so no entries are possible regardless of gate status. MU, RXRX, PANW, CRWD all stay queued. Next fresh deposit routes to DRIP tier first (most underweight) per §17.

thoughts.label: PORTFOLIO HEAVILY STOCK-OVERWEIGHT
thoughts.text: Accumulation stage (portfolio < $5K) targets 20% stock / 50% ETF / 30% DRIP. Current mix is ~74% stock (INTC, NVDA, MRVL) / ~26% ETF (ARKK) / 0% DRIP. Stock is +54pts over target; DRIP is the most underweight tier and gets first claim on new capital.

thoughts.label: IONQ THIRD DOWN SESSION FORMING
thoughts.text: IONQ -2.75% pre-market would make a third consecutive down session after -12.6% over the prior two. Approaching the reassessment trigger; sector-wide quantum rotation, backlog $370M still supports the thesis. Watch only.

learned_patterns.pattern: Fractional positions cannot use conditional sell orders — only market sells, and those require regular hours. A pre-market gap-down into the alert zone (NVDA, MRVL) cannot be acted on until 6:30 AM open, so the between-session monitor + current fractional_positions.json is the only protection in the gap.

AI/semi single-stock positions can swing a position from clear into the stop alert zone in one overnight session on broad risk-off, not company news. NVDA and MRVL both tripped -3% pre-market with no individual catalyst.
Analyst downgrades + scaled insider selling after 200%+ rallies remain durable Gate 2 blockers. DELL (-3.53% again pre-market) and ARM (-5.09%) continue to underperform days after their downgrades.

MU has beaten EPS estimates 8 consecutive quarters (Q3 FY26 +24.3%), but a +15.74% post-earnings spike left it far above any disciplined entry zone. The discipline is to wait for the pullback, not chase the beat.

Early-stage deployment skewed heavily to single stocks (74% vs 20% Accumulation target). Deposit-routing to DRIP/ETF is the non-destructive path back toward target rather than force-selling at a loss.
